0.text: DIFC Courts Rules of Court Order No. 1 of 2017 in Respect of Legal Representation in Hearings Before the Small Claims Tribunal of the DIFC Courts

2.text: of Order No. 1DIFC Courts (/glossary/difc-courts/) Rules (/glossary/rules/) Court (/glossary/court/) of 2017 in Respect of Legal Representation in Hearings Before the Small Claims of the DIFC Courts Tribunal (/glossary/tribunal/)

3.text: I, Michael Hwang, of the DIFC Courts, make the followingChief Justice (/glossary/chief-justice/) Order: In exercise of the powers conferred on me by Article 8(3)(a) of Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004, as amended; and after having reviewed:

4.text: Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004 in respect of the , as amended; Dubai Law No. 12 ofDIFC (/glossary/difc/) 2004 in respect of the Judicial Authority at the DIFC, as amended; DIFC Law No. 10 of 2004 in respect of the DIFC Courts Law; and

5.text: DIFC Order No. 1 of 2014 in respect of the Rules of the DIFC Courts

6.text: 1. This Order may be cited as the Rules of Court Order No. 1 of 2017.

7.text: 2. The Rules of the DIFC Courts (RDC) Part 53.52 and 53.53 shall be amended to read as follows:

11.text: (1) Unless the SCT orders otherwise, a party should present his own case atJudge (/glossary/judge/) a hearing.

12.text: (2) A party may be represented at the hearing by a non-lawyer or lawyer[1] only after obtaining permission from the SCT which is to be given where it appears to the SCT on reasonable grounds that it is necessary in the circumstances.

13.text: (3) If a party is allowed to be represented by a lawyer, or in house Counsel as permitted under Rule 53.53, the opposing party shall be informed and given the opportunity to be represented at the hearing.

14.text: (4) Parties’ attention is drawn to RDC Part 53.70.

17.text: Any of its full-time offi cers or employees (including in house Counsel) may represent a corporate party.” [2]

18.text: 3. This shall fi rst be refl ected in the online version of the RDC, and subsequently in hardcopies due for publication in 2017.

19.text: 4. The amended Rules will come into force on 17 January 2017.

20.text: Michael Hwang Chief Justice of the DIFC Courts

22.text: [1] “Lawyer” in the context of SCT proceedings shall be taken to include DIFC registered as well as Mckenzie friends, which encompasses individuals practitioners (/glossary/practitioner/) who may be legally qualifi ed but not licensed to practice before the DIFC Courts.

23.text: [2] Where a request is made for legal representation at an SCT hearing (as provided for under RDC 53.52 and 53.53), the SCT shall be given 3 working days’ notice of theRegistry (/glossary/registry/) same, so as to enable them to inform the other side accordingly.
